name: Housing Options & Planning Enterprises, INC.
description_short: HUD-approved nonprofit housing counselor providing foreclosure prevention, homebuyer education, financial literacy, and affordable housing development in Maryland.
description_long:
Housing Options & Planning Enterprises (H.O.P.E.) Inc. is a Maryland-based nonprofit organization established to promote equal access to housing through counseling, education, advocacy, and community revitalization. Operating from offices in Oxon Hill and Baltimore, H.O.P.E. serves households seeking to achieve financial stability and sustainable housing solutions.

The organization offers comprehensive housing counseling services including foreclosure prevention counseling for homeowners facing mortgage difficulties, homebuyer education for first-time buyers, financial literacy programs covering budgeting and credit management, and rental counseling for renters. H.O.P.E. also manages HUD-funded affordable housing for elderly individuals and people with disabilities across Prince George's County and Charles County, while operating as a Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) providing consumer loans and USDA 502 Direct financing programs.

H.O.P.E. distinguishes itself through its integrated approach combining personalized one-on-one counseling with group education sessions, its direct involvement in affordable housing development and management rather than just advisory services, and its CDFI status enabling financing tools alongside counseling. The organization explicitly addresses predatory lending education and provides crisis intervention services adapted to community needs.

As a nonprofit focused on underserved populations, H.O.P.E. is best suited for households in financial distress, first-time homebuyers, renters seeking stability, and those seeking free financial counseling. The primary caveat is that some services require appointments and availability may be limited by staff capacity, and certain financing programs (CDFI loans, USDA products) have specific eligibility requirements not detailed on the website.
pros: HUD-approved housing counselor providing free foreclosure prevention counseling with personalized one-on-one crisis intervention
Certified Community Development Financial Institution (CDFI) offering affordable financing options including consumer loans and USDA 502 Direct programs
Operates affordable housing developments and management for elderly and disabled populations, demonstrating community commitment beyond counseling
Comprehensive financial literacy program addressing budgeting, debt reduction, and credit management specific to housing-related decisions
Group education sessions on homebuying process, home maintenance, and predatory lending prevention with practical real-world focus
Dual-location service availability (Oxon Hill and Baltimore) with multiple contact methods (phone, email, website intake)
Holistic mission addressing housing, financial stability, and community revitalization rather than single-issue focus
cons: Baltimore office operates by appointment only, potentially limiting accessibility for walk-in clients or those with urgent needs
Website lacks specific details on counselor certifications, response times, and typical counseling duration, making service expectations unclear
Limited transparency on CDFI financing terms, interest rates, and loan eligibility criteria—consumers must contact directly for details
No online scheduling system visible; requires phone call or email to arrange appointments, adding friction to access
Website does not specify availability windows, wait times, or whether services are available evenings/weekends for working families
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage difficulties seeking personalized intervention without fees
First-time homebuyers in Maryland wanting comprehensive education on the purchase process and predatory lending risks
Renters in need of affordability guidance and tenant rights education to avoid eviction
Individuals seeking free financial literacy and credit improvement counseling as a nonprofit alternative to for-profit providers
diagnosis: H.O.P.E. is best for Maryland residents (particularly those in Prince George's, Charles, and Baltimore counties) facing housing instability, foreclosure risk, or seeking free nonprofit counseling. Primary caveat: services require appointments and some detailed information on financing terms and availability is not published online—prospective clients must contact the organization directly to confirm eligibility and access timelines.
services: Foreclosure prevention counseling with personalized one-on-one guidance
Homebuyer education group sessions on purchase process and home maintenance
Financial literacy programs covering budgeting, debt reduction, and credit management
Rental counseling addressing affordability, tenant rights, and eviction avoidance
Financial capability empowerment program building money management skills
Affordable housing development and management for elderly and disabled populations
HUD-funded housing management in Prince George's County and Charles County
CDFI consumer loan financing for affordable financing needs
Home improvement and facade renovation services (age-in-place support for elderly)
Community development financial institution lending services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 6188 Oxon Hill Rd Ste 700, Oxon Hill, MD 20745
